examples: The immigrant was duped because he trusted everyone
You can't fool me!

meanings.definition: suck or take up or in
examples: A black star absorbs all matter
take in the sights

meanings.definition: call for and obtain payment of
examples: we collected over a million dollars in outstanding debts
he collected the rent
view a show on television
This program will be seen all over the world
view an exhibition
Catch a show on Broadway
see a movie

meanings.definition: express willingness to have in one's home or environs
examples: The community warmly received the refugees
take in the sails
he absorbed the knowledge or beliefs of his tribe

meanings.definition: earn on some commercial or business transaction; earn as salary or wages
examples: How much do you make a month in your new job?
She earns a lot in her new job
this merger brought in lots of money
He clears $5,000 each month

meanings.definition: hear, usually without the knowledge of the speakers
examples: We overheard the conversation at the next table
The cloth takes up the liquid
The sponge absorbs water well
She drew strength from the minister's words

meanings.definition: take up as if with a sponge
serve oneself to, or consume regularly
examples: Have another bowl of chicken soup!
I don't take sugar in my coffee
They adopted two children from Nicaragua
Please take in this skirt--I've lost weight
